title: SOP BGE7.2a: PUBLICATION OF PROTOCOLS
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4foqv","text":"This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) provides general guidance on publishing protocols within the scope of the Biodiversity Genomics Europe (BGE) consortium. BGE is a pan-European consortium that uses genomic methods (e.g., DNA barcoding and whole-genome sequencing) to generate standardised biodiversity data that supports monitoring and conservation action.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":114,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":114,"length":34}],"data":{}},{"key":"35shm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2kim0","text":"This SOP specifically describes how novel protocols from BGE’s Genome Sequencing work stream are documented, curated, and published on SciLifeLab Protocols powered by protocols.io, a web-based platform for sharing and publishing research methods.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":135,"length":20},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":167,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":135,"length":20},{"key":2,"offset":167,"length":12}],"data":{}},{"key":"dj42p","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6fv2m","text":"In brief, protocol publication on this platform begins with the creation of a private protocol in the BGE workspace. The protocol submitter then requests review and approval from a workspace administrator (admin) using the signing flow. If the protocol is ready, the admin proceeds with publication; otherwise, the admin returns it to the submitter for revision. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":102,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":102,"length":13}],"data":{}},{"key":"5v9fg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9jkoe","text":"Once published, the protocol is assigned a DOI (Digital Object Identifier), a persistent unique identifier that should be cited in relevant project outputs (e.g., scientific articles, presentations, reports). The detailed workflow is shown in Figure 1.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2aqmr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cvud1","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":4,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"p7s7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"D48CEA99076111F19A600A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://biodiversitygenomics.eu"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"D48CEAE7076111F19A600A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.scilifelab.se/data-ai/protocols"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"D48CEB27076111F19A600A58A9FEAC02","url":"http://protocols.io"}},"3":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"D48CEB61076111F19A600A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/workspaces/biodiversity-genomics-europe2"}},"4":{"type":"image","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"alt":"","guid":"4406C8660B5411F193660A58A9FEAC02","height":791,"id":720567,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":[{\"key\":\"9d7mh\",\"text\":\"Figure 1. For more detailed guidance, refer to the protocols.io Help Center documentation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":83,"length":20},{"key":1,"offset":151,"length":24}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"54BFCA56DEDF41B78C7DF41167581AAA","url":"https://www.scilifelab.se/data-ai/protocols/"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"2E579EB174B440008297EF743B523224","url":"https://www.protocols.io/help"}}}}
steps: Draft the protocol in an accepted text-based format (e.g., DOCX, PDF, or TXT). It should include: Step-by-step instructions of the research methods Contextual information of the protocol (metadata), such as title, authors, materials, references, safety notes, troubleshooting, among others. Refer to the Protocol Documentation Checklist for further details: SOP_BGE7.2a_v.1.0_Protocol_Documentation_Checklist.docx
Import the document into protocols.io as a private protocol (see guidance) . Start by adding the steps of the protocol and add the contextual information in the respective tabs.
Review the protocol for completeness, clarity, and scientific accuracy. Update as needed.
Use the signing flow to sign and submit the protocol for administrator review. Refer to the guidance on signing and requesting signatures as part of the protocol review and approval process.

A workspace administrator reviews the submitted protocol to verify completeness, accuracy, and compliance with BGE requirements. Refer to the Workspace Administrator Approval Checklist for further details: SOP_BGE7.2a_v1.0_Workspace_Administrator_Approval_Checklist.docx

The administrator determines whether the protocol is ready for publication. Not ready : The protocol is rejected with an explanation and suggested revisions. The submitter revises the protocol and resubmits (return to Step 3 ). Ready : Proceed to Step 8 .

The administrator approves the protocol by signing it. Once signed: The submitter is notified (via email and protocols.io's profile). The administrator publishes the protocol following the protocol publication guidance .

The published protocol is assigned a DOI (a permanent, unique identifier) and becomes publicly accessible. Verify in a web browser that: The protocol record is publicly accessible as intended. The DOI is visible and correct. Record the protocol DOI for citation and use in project materials and reports.
Determine whether the protocol is ready for administrator review. If revisions are still required, return to Step 3 If ready, proceed to Step 5
creator: Angela Fuentes Pardo
